comparison_metadata.rag_query: "DDOS attack mitigation strategies for RAN network tier focusing on high-impact features like udps.srcdst_rst_packet_count, udps.srcdst_syn_packet_count, and udps.syn_packet_count. Include best practices for managing packet counts and optimizing network resilience."

top_documents.title: How can cloud providers use multi-tiered DDoS protection strategies to enhance security?
top_documents.text_preview: Multi-tiered DDoS protection strategies involve implementing several layers of defense to effectively mitigate distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks. Cloud providers can enhance security using ...

primary_actions.reasoning: The RAN tier has the highest contribution (80.6%) to the DDOS prediction, indicating that it is the most affected by the attack. Implementing rate limiting here will help control the volume of incoming requests, thereby mitigating the impact of the attack.

supporting_actions.reasoning: The Edge tier can further assist in filtering out malicious traffic once it has passed through the RAN. Traffic scrubbing will enhance the defense by ensuring that only legitimate traffic reaches the Core, thus complementing the rate limiting action taken at the RAN.

with_rag.overall_reasoning: The RAN is prioritized for the primary action due to its dominant contribution to the DDOS attack, while the Edge supports this action by filtering traffic. The critical threat level and the 100% confidence in the DDOS prediction necessitate immediate action to mitigate the attack effectively.

primary_actions.reasoning: Given the 100% confidence in a DDoS attack, deploying a DDoS Protection Service is essential to absorb and mitigate the attack effectively.

without_rag.overall_reasoning: The predicted DDoS attack is assessed as Critical due to the high confidence level. Immediate action is required to prevent service disruption.
